# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a multi-job GitHub Actions workflow where one job builds artifacts and subsequent jobs download and deploy them. The deployment job should download artifacts from the build job and execute deployment scripts. This is for our multi-stage deployment pipeline.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Multi-Job Deployment Pipeline with GitHub Actions 
on: [push]
jobs:  
# Job builds image artifacts in Docker registry (default) and downloads them after the build is successful   
build_job:      
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest      # use most recent Ubuntu version or can also be other Linux distributions like CentOS, etc.  // specify here if your job should run on specific OS versions  		// You could utilize Node environments too in GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline which are provided by the 'nodes' runner
     steps:      		          # List of tasks that will execute during Job runs   	     	 	       	   			              					                  				 – step name, command to run.                    The specifics depend on your use case               	   // Inline YAML for GitHub Actions -->> yaml(steps:'...')
       - uses: actions/checkout@v2 # If you are using other languages or build tools also replace 'actions/<name>' with the corresponding action in the marketplace    				       		           	         	// Runner image to use, specify as many runner images here per job.      // See more keys and their examples at https://github.com/actions/runner-images   
       - name: Build Image # Specify your needs (e.g., Ubuntu version) Here you can list the specifics required by docker build command 			// This example shows how to use a Dockerfile, we could also pass parameters here if needed  	     // See more at https://github.com/docker-library/docs#using-dockerbuild-image
         with:      				# Store your source and destination info in the context of this step 			// Source can be URL or a git reference for repo, branch or tag; Destination is path to write artifacts after build     // GitHub Actions have ${{ github.workspace }} by default where it stores job parameters      	         				
           source: '.'      # Specify the file that you want docker builder will read (default '.') 					  		           			              	// Source can be URL or a git reference for repo, branch或tag; Destination is path to write artifacts after build     // GitHub Actions have ${{ github.workspace }} by default where it stores job parameters
           # We are not specifying any destination directory as the step will automatically put files in ./.github/workflow		// In this example, if we don't specify a specific location for deployment artifacts then workflow upload them to GitHub repository under ./dir		      // Or you can provide relative path like "artifact_location"    				       
           # This could be the local directory where build product would go. We need Dockerfile inside this dir with commands we want in docker run command 					  			                    		// e.g., CMD [“executable”, ...] to start our application and others as per your requirement   		             
       - name: Deploy Image # Specify what needs to be done after the build job is completed     	        // The use of "env" section in .github/workflows file allows you set variables for later runs  		// In GitHub Actions, we can provide environment via steps like this.
         with:      				# Store your deploy configuration 		                                         					          				                  				            	   	  'VARIABLE=value'     // For example storing deployment related information in env var     	       						        }, {    ...	   })}))))))), the following could be used to set environment variables for later runs.
         env:      				# Store your deploy configuration 		         					                  				           	// If you have specific details, they should go here     // Use '${{ .env }}' syntax if want access it from other actions   	          	 	       	    })
